jobs.job_description:
As a Network Support Analyst at Queensland Airports Limited, you will play a pivotal role in ensuring the seamless operation of our critical network infrastructure across all QAL-managed airports. Your expertise will be instrumental in maintaining network performance, troubleshooting issues, and implementing improvements to support our dynamic and growing operations.

 

Aryan joined the Technology team in 2025 as a Support Analyst and has since moved into a Cyber Security Analyst position. Aryan shares his unique experience working in the QAL team:

“I joined QAL because of its strong emphasis on professionalism and teamwork. What I value most is the collaborative culture, where team members actively share knowledge and support one another’s growth.

QAL provides me with a supportive and motivating environment, and I am proud to be part of the organisation.”

 

How will you make an impact?

We're open to applications from recent graduates, as well as candidates with up to 1-2 years' experience in a similar role. You'll have strong customer service and communication skills, with the ability to assist employees and provide basic service desk support and issue resolution.

Strong organisational and administrative skills are essential, enabling you to coordinate contractor engagement paperwork, manage competing priorities, and ensure tasks are completed accurately and on time.

 

Key deliverables/responsibilities include but are not limited to:

  • Administer and maintain local area networks (LANs), wide area networks (WANs), and associated systems.
  • Perform regular network monitoring to ensure optimal performance and reliability.
  • Troubleshoot and resolve network-related issues promptly to minimize downtime.
  • Implement and manage network security protocols to safeguard against unauthorized access and cyber threats.
  • Ensure compliance with relevant industry standards and regulations.

 

How we support you

At Queensland Airports Limited (QAL), we believe work should support life, not compete with it. Through our Living Well approach, we offer flexible working options for all roles, wellbeing initiatives and a supportive culture so our people can thrive both professionally and personally.

As we continue to grow and transform, we encourage flexible ways of working and open conversations about how work fits alongside life. We also invest in our people through learning, mentoring and career pathways across our four airports.

 

What we’ve built together

At QAL, our people connect communities. We own and operate Gold Coast, Townsville, Mount Isa and Longreach airports, linking regional Queensland and northern New South Wales with the rest of Australia and the world.

More than 8 million passengers travel through our airports each year, supported by teams working together to create safe, welcoming and efficient experiences. Our vision is to be the first-choice gateway for our regions, creating valuable connections and inspiring a sustainable future.

We’re committed to an inclusive and psychologically safe workplace where people feel valued, supported and encouraged to be themselves. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds and experiences, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, and encourage you to apply even if you don’t meet every requirement.

QAL is a WORK180 Endorsed Employer, independently recognised for supporting long‑term careers, flexibility, wellbeing and equal opportunity. Learn more about working at QAL here.

The successful candidate will be required to adhere to QAL’s Drug and Alcohol Management Plan (DAMP), which includes pre-employment Drug and Alcohol Testing and have the ability to obtain an Aviation Security Identification Card (ASIC), which requires the completion of a Police Check.

Please obtain the Position Description by clicking HERE, or if you are on Seek, by copying the following location into your browser to access the Position Description through our careers site: https://qal.elmotalent.com.au/careers/QALcareers/jobshttp://www.qldairports.com.au/.

jobs.job_title: IT Project Manager | Learning Systems | 6 Month Contract
jobs.job_description:
IT Project Manager: Limelight People have partnered with a leading organisation within the education sector to recruit an experienced IT Project Manager for an initial 6 month contract, with a strong likelihood of extension. This is an exciting opportunity to join a high-performing technology division of over 60 IT professionals, delivering strategic Learning Systems initiatives that have a direct impact on the education experience.

Why you'll love this opportunity
  • Excellent daily rates with an initial 6-month contract and strong extension potential.
  • Hybrid working environment offering the flexibility to work from both home and the office.
  • Lead meaningful technology projects that support enterprise Learning Systems within a collaborative and forward-thinking IT team.
IT Project Manager: You will lead the successful delivery of multiple technology projects across the organisation's Learning Systems portfolio. Working closely with business stakeholders, vendors and technical delivery teams, you will drive project outcomes from initiation through to delivery, ensuring projects are delivered on time, within budget and aligned to business objectives.

IT Project Manager: Key responsibilities include:
  • Leading end-to-end delivery of technology and business transformation projects.
  • Developing project plans, schedules, budgets and risk management strategies.
  • Managing project governance, reporting and executive stakeholder communication.
  • Coordinating cross-functional teams including Business Analysts, Developers, Architects and Vendors.
  • Driving Agile and hybrid project delivery methodologies.
  • Managing project scope, dependencies, issues and change requests.
  • Ensuring successful implementation of enterprise Learning Systems and related technology initiatives.
IT Project Manager: To be successful, you'll ideally have experience with:
  • End-to-end IT Project Management within complex enterprise environments.
  • Delivery of enterprise applications, business systems or digital transformation projects.
  • Strong stakeholder engagement across executive, business and technical teams.
  • Agile, Waterfall or Hybrid project delivery methodologies.
  • Budget, resource, risk and vendor management.
  • Project governance and executive reporting.
  • Experience delivering Learning Systems, Education Technology or curriculum-related projects will be highly regarded.
IT Project Manager: This is an outstanding opportunity to join a leading education organisation undertaking significant investment across its technology landscape. If you're an experienced Project Manager who enjoys delivering complex enterprise projects while working in a collaborative, purpose-driven environment, we'd love to hear from you.
If your experience aligns with the above, please apply today.

jobs.job_description:
Enterprise Solution Architect - 6 month contract

Are you an experienced Enterprise or Solution Architect looking for the opportunity to build an architecture capability from the ground up?

Limelight People is partnering with a large, Gold Coast based organisation seeking an Enterprise Solution Architect tasked with establishing and embedding a modern architecture practice across its enterprise technology environment.

This is an influential role where you'll develop the frameworks, artefacts and governance that will shape future technology decisions across the organisation. 

About the Role

Working within a collaborative technology division of approximately 60 IT professionals, you'll partner closely with Business Solutions, Operations, Cyber Security and Infrastructure teams to improve the way technology is designed, governed and evolved.

Your primary objective will be to establish the organisation's architecture capability by developing practical frameworks, standards and governance that become embedded into day-to-day IT delivery.

Key responsibilities include:

  • Establish and embed the enterprise solutions architecture practice
  • Develop architecture artefacts, standards, templates and design guidelines
  • Build and maintain architecture principles, reference architectures and decision frameworks
  • Assess and report on enterprise systems health, lifecycle, technical debt and technology risk
  • Improve visibility of application, integration and service dependencies
  • Establish pragmatic architecture governance and Design Authority processes
  • Define integration patterns, API standards and data flow models
  • Support technology projects, procurement activities and strategic investment decisions
  • Coach and mentor technical teams to build architecture capability across the organisation

About You - You'll be a pragmatic architect who enjoys balancing strategic thinking with hands-on delivery.

You'll ideally bring:

  • Microsoft Azure and Microsoft 365
  • ServiceNow or enterprise ITSM platforms
  • Cyber security architecture and governance
  • Experience within large, multi-stakeholder organisations
  • Extensive experience as an Enterprise Architect or Senior Solution Architect
  • Experience establishing or maturing an architecture practice within a complex enterprise environment
  • Strong knowledge of enterprise applications, SaaS, cloud platforms and integration architecture
  • Experience developing architecture frameworks, standards and governance models
  • Strong stakeholder engagement skills with the ability to influence technical and executive audiences
  • Experience assessing application portfolios, system health, lifecycle management and technology risk
  • Familiarity working within Agile delivery environments

Please apply if you're passionate about building architecture capability, improving enterprise technology landscapes and influencing strategic technology outcomes.

The Role...
Working across custom APIs, the in house CRM, and the rollout of new POS features, you will get hands on with both front end and backend work. This is a role for someone who wants to be trusted with real problems early in their career, rather than sitting on the sidelines.

Why This Role...
This is a genuine opportunity to build real ownership early in your career, working across meaningful projects rather than maintenance tickets.   • Strong PHP, plus HTML, CSS and JavaScript, with genuine custom theme and plugin development experience
  • Experience integrating payment gateways and third party APIs into WooCommerce
  • A solid grasp of front end performance, accessibility and SEO fundamentals
  • Comfortable working with Git and a controlled release process
  • Graduate level through to 2 plus years experience, ideal for someone ready to take real ownership early in their career

Who you are:

  • Strong full-stack experience: .NET backend and modern JavaScript frameworks (React, TypeScript)

  • Strong expertise in Entity Framework, SQL, data modelling, and OOP principles
  • Desirable: Experience with Dynamics 365 Business Central, test automation, AWS, and observability tools (e.g., Grafana)

We're entering a new chapter for the Chief Technology Office at the City of Gold Coast as we deliver bold, future-focused solutions. We are seeking an experienced and proactive Incident and Problem Analyst to support the effective handling, resolution and analysis of ICT incidents and problems across the organisation.

This role plays a critical part in restoring normal service operations as quickly as possible, identifying root causes, and preventing recurrence — ensuring high availability, reliability and strong user satisfaction across ICT services.

Working within an ITIL-aligned environment, you will contribute to operational excellence and continuous service improvement.

As the Incident and Problem Analyst, you will monitor, coordinate and analyse incidents across the ICT environment. You will ensure accurate categorisation and prioritisation in line with SLAs, collaborate with technical teams to drive timely resolution, and support structured root cause analysis for recurring issues.

You will maintain problem and known error records, generate performance reporting, and actively contribute to service improvement initiatives.

This role requires strong coordination, stakeholder engagement and analytical capability, particularly in high-priority or high-impact situations.

We’re looking for people who have:

  • 5+ years’ ICT experience in an IT service operational support role within an ITIL environment.
  • Strong ICT coordination and operational management skills.
  • Excellent communication and negotiation skills.
  • Strong stakeholder engagement capability across technical and business teams.
  • Demonstrated leadership capability in managing incidents and driving resolution outcomes.

Desirable:

  • Prince2, Agile, Six Sigma, Lean and/or Cyber Security experience or certifications.

The Opportunity

We have an exciting opportunity for an experienced Business Analyst to join our Digital, Technology & Innovation team and play a central role in delivering SEE Group's digital transformation program. You'll bridge the gap between business needs and technology solutions - engaging stakeholders across the business, capturing and analysing requirements, mapping current and future-state processes, and translating these into clear, well-documented specifications that guide the design and delivery of new systems and enhancements.

Working across a broad application landscape spanning enterprise systems, Microsoft cloud platforms, and bespoke applications, you'll help ensure the solutions we deliver are fit for purpose, adopted successfully, and deliver measurable value to the business.

Responsibilities

ABOUT STACKED FARM

Stacked Farm is revolutionizing fresh produce as Australia's first end-to-end automated indoor vertical farming company. Operating across Australia (Gold Coast and Melbourne) and the United States, we combine robotics, automation, software and engineering to produce premium leafy greens year-round. Technology is at the heart of everything we do, from enterprise IT infrastructure and cloud services through to operational technology (OT), industrial networking and cybersecurity.

As we continue to grow, we're looking for a passionate technology enthusiast to join our team and build a rewarding career in IT.

ABOUT THE ROLE

The Junior IT Systems Administrator provides essential IT support across our multi-site operations, ensuring day-to-day technology systems, hardware and software operate reliably.

This role combines IT support, endpoint provisioning and junior systems administration, making it an excellent opportunity for someone early in their IT career who wants to develop into a Systems Administrator.

Reporting to the Chief Technology Officer, you'll provide responsive user support while progressively taking ownership of systems administration, infrastructure and networking responsibilities. As your skills develop, you'll gain exposure to operational technology (OT), cloud and on-premises infrastructure, cybersecurity, enterprise networking and IT infrastructure design.

No two days are the same, and you'll work alongside experienced technical professionals in an environment where you'll continually learn and develop your skills.

RESPONSIBILITIES

  • Provide responsive Level 1 and Level 2 IT support through our ticketing system, troubleshooting hardware, software and user issues across multiple sites.

  • Configure, deploy and maintain laptops, desktops, mobile devices and peripherals.

  • Provision and manage user accounts, access permissions and endpoint devices as part of team member onboarding, offboarding and role changes.

  • Assist with the administration of Windows and Linux servers, virtualisation platforms and remote monitoring and management systems.

  • Support Google Applications and other core business applications.

  • Assist with network administration including switching, routing, VLANs, VPNs, wireless networking and firewall management.

  • Support operational technology (OT) networking, including network segmentation, access control and industrial network hardening.

  • Participate in cybersecurity activities including patch management, endpoint protection, vulnerability remediation and security best practices.

  • Maintain IT documentation, network diagrams, asset registers and standard operating procedures.

  • Assist with hardware procurement, lifecycle management and software licensing.

  • Contribute to infrastructure improvements, automation initiatives and technical projects as your experience grows.

ABOUT YOU

You're passionate about technology and enjoy solving problems. Whether you've gained experience through work, study, certifications or personal projects, you're always looking to learn something new.

You take pride in providing excellent customer service, enjoy troubleshooting technical issues and are excited by the opportunity to grow into a Systems Administrator role.

REQUIREMENTS

  • Diploma, Advanced Diploma or Degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, Network Administration or a related discipline (or currently completing).

  • Experience in IT support, helpdesk or junior systems administration is advantageous.

  • Solid understanding of networking fundamentals including TCP/IP, subnetting, VLANs, routing, switching and firewall concepts.

  • Working knowledge of Google applications and basic Linux administration.

  • Understanding of virtualisation concepts and hypervisor technologies such as VMware or Hyper-V.

  • Strong analytical and troubleshooting skills with a logical approach to problem solving.

  • Excellent communication skills with the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical users.

  • High attention to detail and strong organisational skills.

  • Proactive, reliable and able to work both independently and collaboratively.

  • Genuine passion for technology with a strong interest in networking, systems administration and infrastructure.

  • Australian working rights.

  • Must be based on the Gold Coast.

jobs.job_description:
About us

The G:link is operated and maintained by Keolis Gold Coast (KGC). Keolis Gold Coast is the proud subsidiary of Keolis Australia and New Zealand, a leading private provider of multi-modal public transport in Australia.

Some of our Benefits

  • Salary Packaging (inc. salary sacrificing additional superannuation and novated car leasing);
  • Education and learning opportunities;
  • Employee Assistance Program (for employees and immediate family);
  • Reward & Recognition (inc. years of service, employee of the year, informal recognition);
  • Health & Wellness Programs (inc. team challenges, Fitness Passport);
  • Flexible work options (including Paid Parental and Adoptive Leave);
  • Onsite parking; and
  • Enjoy a great work life balance on the Gold Coast!

Our Culture 

What makes working with us so special is our culture, one that embraces our Keolis core values (We Care, We Imagine, We Commit) and shared commitment to the development of high performing natural leaders within the organisation. We are proud to recruit, train and retain the most talented professionals on the Gold Coast, who share our customer (Think Like a Passenger) and safety focused (Zero Harm) approach. Our staff are at the core of what we do, and by cultivating the right environment and values, we have an engaged workforce with a sense of team.

About the Role

The Data and Reporting Analyst - Parental Leave Cover position will see you maintain accurate data collection and reporting to enable early detection of key performance indicator trends and corrections, while meeting daily, weekly and monthly reporting deadlines.

Position Responsibilities

· Develop and provide reports on key performance indicators as required;

· Quality control and auditing of data entries into the various system registers;

· Address any quality issues with the source/root cause;

· Analyse, cleanse, and transform the user data into usable information, then align the data to business reporting requirements; 

·Liaise with external data providers to ensure the quality of the data that Keolis Gold Coast (KGC) receives meets their reporting requirements;

· Liaise with all business departments and management levels to ensure the quality of the data meets the reporting requirements;

· Design reports and develop metrics to analyse and help drive business decisions on identified areas requiring attention;

· Assist with the continued growth and development of the transition to digital reporting;

· Support the Scheduling and Events Coordinator as needed in the data and administration tasks.

Competencies Required

· Essential: Proven advanced knowledge in Microsoft Word, Excel, PowerPoint;

· Essential: Experience with producing reports;

· Favourable, but not essential: experience with PowerBI;

· Understanding of how to interpret information/data and graphs;

· Ability to create performance improvement plans;

· Ability to clearly express processes and information.

Profile Requirements

· Fluent in English with excellent written and verbal communication skills;

· Quick and precise with data entry;

· Ability to adapt to ever changing reporting requirements and environments;

· Ability to challenge ideas and processes confidently;

· Relied upon to work with own initiative as well as collectively in a team;

· Robust and resilient in regard to disposition;

· Able to display high levels of attention to detail and vigilance;

· Able to identify and rectify problems and issues in a timely manner.

As part of the recruitment processes at KGC, pre-employment checks include National Police Certificate and Rail Safety Worker Pre-employment Medical. Further to this, successful candidates will be required to provide a National Police Certificate where requested throughout employment and maintain their Rail Safety Medical status. Given the nature of the position you have applied for and, in particular, the duties and responsibilities that it includes, KGC considers this to be a reasonable requirement. KGC will assess a candidate’s and employee’s criminal record history against the inherent requirements of the relevant position before making a determination as to their suitability or otherwise for the role. 

Keolis is committed to creating a workplace that builds respect, promotes diversity and encourages inclusiveness. 

Candidates who have applied for this position previously are requested not to reapply, as duplicate applications will not be considered.

Company Overview 

Veracross unifies school communities with world-class information systems that improve the quality of education and enhance learning. We support K-12 private and independent schools with a one-record platform that connects school operations, communication, and data across departments. Trusted by more than 3,200 schools in over 60 countries, Veracross is known for reliable software, strong data security, and high-quality customer support. 

In Australia, New Zealand, and the wider Asia Pacific region, Veracross supports 800+ schools across Veracross, Digistorm, Firefly, and ePraise. The APAC team is based in Australia and offers in-time-zone access through Support, Implementation, and Customer Success, alongside regional technical and product staff. 

Role Summary 

The CSM Team lead, manages the Asia Pacific Customer Success team and is accountable for team performance across retention, qualified opportunity generation, and SIS lead creation. This role owns team coaching, churn forecasting, renewal execution, and operational consistency across the regional portfolio. 

The CSM Team Lead directly leads a team of three Customer Success Managers covering the APAC book, which spans roughly 800 customers across Australia, New Zealand, and Asia Pacific international schools. The book is concentrated in the Australian and New Zealand independent and Catholic school markets, with a smaller set of higher-value international school accounts. This is a high-volume regional portfolio, and the CSM Team Lead is accountable for building coverage discipline and team capacity against it. 

Partnering closely with Sales, Support, Implementation, Product, and leadership to reduce churn, expand customer value, and build a high-performing team. This role may retain a very limited strategic portfolio, but its primary focus is team leadership and regional execution. 

Job Responsibilities 

  • Lead, coach, and develop the APAC CSM team across Australia, New Zealand, and Asia Pacific accounts.
  • Set clear expectations tied to retention, qualified opportunity generation, SIS lead creation, and account management discipline. 
  • Own regional churn forecasting and lead regular risk reviews so risks are identified early, documented clearly, and actively mitigated. 
  • Manage span-of-control and coverage in a high-volume book, balancing account distribution across the team and surfacing capacity and coverage gaps to leadership with recommendations. 
  • Step into high-risk or high-value customer situations as needed to support save plans, renewals, and executive alignment. 
  • Ensure renewals are managed proactively, forecasted accurately, and documented cleanly in Salesforce. 
  • Drive expansion discipline by coaching CSMs on opportunity identification, lead quality, and effective partnership with Sales, including Digistorm cross-sell and Magnus and Firefly motions where relevant. 
  • Enforce consistent use of Salesforce and Catalyst.io for health monitoring, risk tracking, task management, and account planning. 
  • Partner cross-functionally with Sales, Support, Implementation, and Product to resolve escalations and improve customer outcomes. 
  • Hire, onboard, manage, and develop talent, including succession planning for future Team Leads and Managers. 
  • Provide regular reporting and recommendations on regional risk, team performance, process gaps, and staffing needs. 

Requirements

  • Bachelor's Degree or equivalent experience. 
  • 3-5 years of experience in SaaS customer success, account management, or other post-sales roles. 
  • 2+ years of experience in management position 
  • Demonstrated success driving retention, renewals, and expansion performance. 
  • Strong experience with churn forecasting, pipeline management, and operational rigor. 
  • Demonstrated ability to manage a high-volume book or team coverage model, where the challenge is prioritisation and coverage across many accounts rather than deep focus on a few. 
  • Strong coaching, performance management, and cross-functional leadership skills. 
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills. 
  • Ability to operate strategically while staying close to execution details. 
  • Knowledge of the Australian and New Zealand independent and Catholic school markets strongly preferred, including familiarity with the academic calendar (four terms, late-January to mid-December school year), school leadership structures, and associations such as AISNSW, AHISA, and state Catholic education offices. Experience in K-12, education SaaS, or a related niche market is preferred; candidates from outside the sector must show evidence of learning the unwritten rules of a specialised market quickly. 
  • Comfortable operating across time zones and currencies, supporting both AUD/NZD domestic accounts and a smaller set of USD-billed international school accounts. 

Benefits

The best tools to get the job done!

When you join Veracross, you get a Macbook Pro, a 32-inch monitor, and an adjustable desk and chair for the office. There's also an allowance for your setup at home to ensure you're comfortable working there.

Growth opportunities!

Veracross is growing fast, allowing you to own your role and grow with us! Operating within a global team, there are opportunities for professional development allowing your skills to grow as the business grows.

Work/life balance

Management encourages taking hour-long lunches and finishing on time and understands that you have a life outside of work. You can take the day off for your birthday and get a $100-a-year allowance for well-being. Massage, anyone?

Parental leave policy

As part of Veracross parental leave policy, you’re eligible for paid leave as a primary parent and secondary parent when you welcome a new child into your family. This is available after 12 months in a full-time role.

Flexibility

Veracross offers a flexible working environment, hybrid options.

Make a difference

Our work makes a positive contribution to our schools, their students and their families around the world!

Snacks, drinks & coffee

Digistorm’s (Veracross) Gold Coast HQ offers a coffee machine, snack bar and fully stocked drinks fridge to keep energy levels high! Vege chips, kombucha, almond milk, cookies; you name it, they have it.

Culture

The Veracross team is fun, relaxed, and sociable, reflected in the casual dress code and social events. The office has an open floor plan, which allows for cross-team communication and a collaborative environment. Join some staff for lunchtime surfing or hit up nearby food spots for a burger by the beach.

Be Bold. Belong. Be More.

At Southern Cross University, we do things differently, and so can you.

A career with us is more than you might expect. Yes, it’s about the heart, connectedness and community you’d hope for in a regional university. But there’s also so much more. Our game changing Southern Cross Model brings a deeper, focused learning experience and our research is deeply connected to our regions, making a difference on a global scale. This is your chance to help us build something new and meaningful for our students, our communities and your career. To drive change, make a real impact, and thrive in a place as unique as you.

You can be good anywhere. Here, you can be great.

Your opportunity

The Office of Business Intelligence and Quality (BIQ) is seeking a Data Coordinator to manage ad-hoc data requests and data reporting in the Business Intelligence (BI) team. In this role, you'll be the first point of contact for data requests and the face of the BI team. You will work with stakeholders at all levels of the University to deliver trusted data, reports and insights. Your work will directly support accreditations, reviews, university governance and strategic decision-making.

You will make an impact by:

  • Receiving, managing and completing data requests with expert assistance from your team
  • Delivering high-quality customer service to clients from all parts of the University.
  • Producing accurate data reports using analytical software, to support course and Faculty reviews, strategic initiatives, external accreditations and institutional compliance.
  • Compiling, reviewing and validating data reports while maintaining exceptional accuracy and attention to detail.
  • Building strong relationships across the University by translating student data into meaningful information for stakeholders to use.

What you will bring

You enjoy solving problems, working with data and connecting people with the information they need. You're highly organised and take pride in producing accurate work. Most importantly, you're curious, eager to learn and enjoy developing new technical skills.

To succeed in this role, you will also need:

  • Completion of a relevant degree with subsequent relevant work experience or equivalent combination of relevant skills, knowledge and experience
  • An understanding of how university student data is collected, processed and stored, or demonstrated experience processing or analysing administrative data.
  • Strong Microsoft Excel (or similar spreadsheet) skills and confidence working with formulas and large datasets The ability to extract, transform and analyse data.
  • High-level problem-solving skills with exceptional attention to detail and a demonstrated ability to follow complex procedures.
  • Outstanding customer service and communication skills, with the ability to clarify client requirements, and give clear explanations of data to stakeholders verbally and in writing.

This role requires some use of analytical software packages including R and Power BI. If you don't have experience with these tools but can demonstrate your data skills, excellent attention to detail and a willingness to learn, we strongly encourage you to apply. For the right applicant, comprehensive on-the-job training and support is available to help you advance your skills.

Why Southern Cross University

SCU is proudly a regional university, young, small and vibrant. That means you’ll find more exposure, opportunity and ownership - to grow, achieve and build a career that means more. And you’ll do it alongside passionate people and leaders who go the extra mile to help you succeed. A career with us is about more than just work. It’s about place, community and the life you want to live. With three main campuses at the Gold Coast, Coffs Harbour and the Northern Rivers region of NSW, we’re integral to all these wonderful communities. Find your place and be more in this beautiful part of the world.

Your opportunity is:

  • Full-time, fixed term (12 months)
  • Located at either Gold Coast, QLD 4225, or Lismore, NSW 2480.
  • HEW Level 6. The base salary ranges from $95,961 - $105,250 per annum, plus 17% employer’s contribution to superannuation. The total remuneration package is up to $123,143.

You will enjoy a range of perks designed to support your wellbeing, growth and work-life balance. For more information on our benefits and what it’s like to work at SCU, visit Why work at Southern Cross University?

Be more with Southern Cross University today

Southern Cross University values potential as much as experience. If you do not meet all the listed requirements but are excited about the role and confident you can contribute, we encourage you to apply or reach out for more information.

About the team

We're entering a new chapter for the Chief Technology Office at the City of Gold Coast as we deliver bold, future-focused technology solutions. With the launch of our IT Operating Model and the transition of critical IT services in-house, we're transforming the way we deliver technology making it faster, more responsive and better aligned to the needs of our organisation and community.

As part of the Enterprise Data & Analytics Platform team, you'll help shape the City's modern data landscape by supporting the platforms that underpin enterprise reporting, analytics and data-driven decision making. About the role

As the Platform Administrator, you will be responsible for the administration, optimisation and ongoing evolution of the City's Microsoft Fabric environment.

Working closely with our Azure Administrators, Purview Administrator, Data Engineering Team, and respective Data Analytics teams you will help deliver a modern enterprise platform that enables trusted, self-service analytics and data-driven decision-making across the City.

jobs.job_title: Founding Engineer – Full-Stack (Node.js, Angular, AWS & AI)
jobs.job_description:
Tenderfy is redefining how construction and infrastructure companies prepare winning tenders.

We've built a platform that combines great workflows, intelligent automation and AI to eliminate the chaos, duplication and manual effort that has traditionally plagued tendering.

Customers are saving significant time, improving tender quality and changing the way their teams work. We're growing quickly and looking for exceptional people to help build what's next.

The market is responding.

Customers are onboarding, revenue is growing, and we're now looking for a Founding Engineer to help shape the next stage of the company.

This isn't a maintenance role.

This isn't a role where you'll spend your days working through a backlog someone else created.

This is a seat at the table.

You'll have direct influence over architecture, product direction, engineering standards, AI strategy and how we scale the platform over the coming years.

If you're looking for a place where your work genuinely matters, keep reading.

What You'll Be Building

You'll work across the entire platform, including:

  • Backend development using Node.js and TypeScript

  • Modern Angular front-end development

  • AWS infrastructure and cloud architecture

  • AI-powered workflows and automation

  • RAG, vector databases and LLM integrations

  • Document generation and processing pipelines

  • Performance, scalability and platform reliability

  • Integrations with enterprise platforms and third-party systems

  • Internal tooling to improve engineering velocity

You'll work directly alongside the founder and collaborate with our offshore engineering team to rapidly deliver improvements and new capabilities.

What Success Looks Like

Within your first 12 months, you'll:

  • Become a key technical decision-maker within the business

  • Improve platform performance, reliability and scalability

  • Help shape Tenderfy's AI roadmap

  • Strengthen engineering processes and release velocity

  • Contribute to building a world-class engineering culture

  • Play a major role in scaling Tenderfy nationally and beyond

What We're Looking For

We're less interested in years of experience and more interested in capability.

You'll likely have strong experience in:

  • Node.js and modern TypeScript

  • Angular or similar modern front-end frameworks

  • AWS infrastructure and cloud-native development

  • API design and integrations

  • Software architecture and scalable systems

  • AI tooling and modern developer workflows

Bonus points if you've worked in a start-up, scale-up or high-growth environment.

Who You Are

You're commercially minded.

You care about solving customer problems, not just writing code.

You take ownership.

You see problems and instinctively look for ways to improve them.

You actively use tools like Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Cursor, GitHub Copilot and AI-assisted development workflows to move faster and produce better outcomes.

You enjoy building.

You enjoy shipping.

You enjoy learning.

Most importantly, you're excited by the idea of helping build something meaningful from the ground up.

Why Join Tenderfy

  • Meaningful equity opportunity

  • Real ownership and autonomy

  • Direct influence on product and architecture

  • Fast-moving environment with minimal bureaucracy

  • Work on cutting-edge AI applications

  • Build software used by some of Australia's leading construction and infrastructure businesses

  • Help shape the future of an ambitious Australian SaaS company

We're building for the long term and we're looking for someone who wants to be part of that journey.

If that sounds like you, we'd love to talk.

Apply with your resume, GitHub profile, portfolio, or simply send through something you've built that you're proud of.

About Buildsafe

Buildsafe is one of Australia's leading construction technology companies, delivering innovative software, AI-powered products and critical infrastructure that help improve safety, productivity and visibility across thousands of construction sites nationwide. Technology is at the centre of everything we do. As our BuildCam platform continues to grow, so does the infrastructure that supports it.

About the Role
We're looking for an
Infrastructure & Systems Administrator to join our IT team, working alongside our Senior Infrastructure Administrator and reporting to the CIO. You'll play a key role in supporting and evolving the infrastructure that powers both Buildsafe and BuildCam.

This is a hands-on role combining enterprise systems administration with physical infrastructure deployment. From commissioning servers, expanding storage and configuring networks to managing Windows Server, Docker, IIS and Microsoft 365 environments, no two days are the same.

Key Responsibilities

  • Build, deploy and maintain enterprise server, storage and network infrastructure.

  • Administer Windows Server, Active Directory, Group Policy, DNS, IIS and Microsoft 365.

  • Deploy and manage Docker containers and hosted applications.

  • Monitor infrastructure performance, security, backups and disaster recovery.

  • Configure and support switches, firewalls, VPNs and networking equipment.

  • Troubleshoot infrastructure issues and support branch deployments.

  • Contribute to infrastructure projects, automation, documentation and continuous improvement.

AI-First Culture

AI is embedded in how we work. We encourage our IT team to use AI to automate repetitive tasks, troubleshoot faster, generate documentation, develop scripts and improve operational efficiency. We're looking for someone who enjoys learning, embraces new technology and is always looking for smarter ways to work.

About You

You'll ideally have:

  • Permanent working rights in Australia (essential)

  • 3+ years' experience in Infrastructure or Systems Administration.

  • Strong Windows Server, Active Directory, DNS and Group Policy experience.

  • Experience with IIS, Docker and Microsoft 365 administration.

  • Hands-on experience with enterprise server hardware and NAS/storage platforms (Synology highly regarded).

  • Solid networking knowledge (switches, VLANs, VPNs and firewalls).

  • PowerShell or scripting experience.

  • Strong troubleshooting, documentation and organisational skills.

  • A passion for AI, automation and continuous improvement.

What Buildsafe Can Offer You

Join a scaling division backed by an established national brand, where your contributions genuinely make an impact.

  • Permanent full-time role – salary paid weekly

  • Office based role, Monday to Friday

  • Corporate uniform allowance

  • Fortnightly professional car cleaning on site

  • Regular team bonding experiences

  • Access to our Staff Care program and Employee Assistance Program

  • Discounted gym memberships through Fitness Passport

  • A culture that values innovation, safety, and collaboration

About Us

Buildsafe is one of Australia's leading construction technology companies, delivering innovative software, AI-powered products and critical infrastructure that help improve safety, productivity and visibility across thousands of construction sites nationwide.

Technology is at the centre of everything we do. As our BuildCam platform continues to grow, so does the infrastructure that supports it.

About the Role

Our BuildCam camera system provides a flexible and reliable solution for site monitoring. With 24/7 supervision of residential construction sites, our customers and stakeholders can track developments in real time.

In this newly created role, we are seeking an experienced IT Support Officer to join our expanding BuildCam team. This position plays a key role in maintaining and supporting BuildCam’s technology and IT infrastructure. You’ll be responsible for the setup, troubleshooting, and monitoring of BuildCam systems, as well as footage and data management, software and hardware updates, and both customer and internal support.

The successful candidate will provide integral support to all aspects of BuildCam’s IT systems and services, both onsite and remotely. This position is hands-on, dynamic, and suited to someone who thrives in a problem-solving environment and enjoys working both independently and as part of a collaborative team.

Your Core Responsibilities

  • Provide onsite and remote technical support to internal teams and clients

  • Set up, configure, and troubleshoot BuildCam camera systems, modems, and solar monitoring equipment

  • Conduct quality assurance checks and ensure systems meet performance standards

  • Manage system updates, maintenance, and asset tracking

  • Monitor power, data, and connectivity performance across all BuildCam systems

  • Provide guidance and technical support via phone, email, or chat

  • Assist in continuous improvement by refining processes and identifying optimisation opportunities

About You

You’ll have recent experience working in a similar IT support role, with relevant qualifications in information technology or a related discipline. You’re someone who can work within defined timeframes, manage multiple priorities, and collaborate effectively with internal stakeholders to achieve great outcomes.

You’ll also bring:

  • Experience on a Service Desk or as an IT Support Officer

  • Strong troubleshooting and diagnostic skills (hardware, software, and networks)

  • Basic knowledge of server and networking hardware

  • Understanding of network setup, connectivity, and troubleshooting

  • Experience conducting quality assurance checks on technical systems

  • Ability to monitor system performance, data integrity, and security alerts

  • Excellent communication and customer service skills

  • Working knowledge of Microsoft Windows server and desktop operating systems

What Buildsafe Can Offer You

Join a scaling division backed by an established national brand, where your contributions genuinely make an impact.

  • Monday to Friday, officed based

  • Corporate uniform allowance

  • Fortnightly professional car cleaning on site

  • Regular team bonding experiences

  • Access to our Staff Care program and Employee Assistance Program

  • Discounted gym memberships through Fitness Passport

Through our education brands Arcadia College, Arcadia College (Distance Education), Ohana College and Ohana Academy, we provide safe, future-focused learning environments that help students reconnect and thrive.

As we continue to grow, we are seeking an experienced ICT Technician to provide exceptional technical support and maintain high system standards across our campuses. Although based at these locations, ICT Technicians may be required to travel to and work from either our Logan locations or our Gold Coast locations at Varsity Lakes or Robina. 

Why Join Ohana for Youth?

  • Join a supportive, purpose-led team

  • PBI salary packaging through SPA (Charity / Not-for-Profit status) – take home more pay and reduce tax

  • Employee Assistance Program for staff and families

Our Commitment to Child Safety

Ohana for Youth is committed to promoting a safe, inclusive and culturally responsive environment for all students, ensuring their safety and wellbeing is prioritised in every aspect of the organisations operations. We recognise our fundamental duty of care and are fully committed to the safety and wellbeing of all children and young people in our community. Operating within an organization-wide Apple device and Google Workspace environment, you will manage helpdesk tickets, maintain hardware, and ensure our network infrastructure runs seamlessly.

Your key responsibilities will include:

Requirements:

jobs.job_title: Software Test Automation and DevOps Engineer
jobs.job_description:
An exciting opportunity has become available to join Siemens as a Software Test Automation & DevOps Engineer within our expanding OMVS R&D Team.

This newly created position, based in Yatala QLD, comes at a pivotal time for the business, with continued growth, strong project demand, and ongoing investment in innovative energy infrastructure technologies. Reporting directly to the OMVS R&D Manager, you will play a key role in delivering secure, reliable, and high-quality software solutions across our medium voltage power distribution and protection product portfolio.

This is a hands-on technical role offering exposure to a broad range of technologies including:

  • Embedded firmware for protection and communications devices
  • Desktop applications
  • Mobile applications (iOS/Android)
  • Cloud-based SaaS platforms
  • AWS cloud infrastructure and security environments

Working closely with software development, product management, and customer care teams, you will take ownership across software testing, CI/CD pipelines, release management, cloud infrastructure, and ongoing system reliability. You will thrive in a collaborative environment where teamwork, continuous improvement, and innovation are highly valued, with the opportunity to contribute fresh ideas and help shape the future direction of our products and processes.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Design, develop, and maintain automated test frameworks across multiple platforms
  • Execute both automated and manual testing for software and hardware-integrated systems
  • Build and maintain Azure DevOps CI/CD pipelines
  • Manage development, staging, and production deployment environments
  • Drive end-to-end release management and deployment processes
  • Monitor and secure AWS cloud infrastructure environments
  • Implement and improve monitoring, logging, and security practices
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure successful product delivery and software quality

About You:

You are a proactive and solutions-focused professional who enjoys working as part of a team, sharing knowledge, and contributing new ideas. You bring a strong sense of ownership, a collaborative mindset, and a passion for delivering high-quality outcomes in technically complex environments.

You will ideally bring experience across:

  • Automated testing frameworks such as Selenium, Appium, Pytest, JUnit or Robot Framework
  • Azure DevOps and CI/CD pipeline management
  • AWS services including EC2, S3, Lambda, RDS, VPC and CloudWatch
  • Python or other scripting/programming languages
  • Git and modern software development practices
  • DevOps, release management, and cloud security principles

Experience within embedded systems, electrical infrastructure, utilities, energy, or industrial technology environments will be highly regarded.

Why Join Siemens?

This is an outstanding opportunity to join a collaborative and forward-thinking R&D team working on technologies that support critical electrical infrastructure globally. You’ll be part of a business experiencing strong momentum in the market, with the opportunity to contribute to innovative products while continuing to grow and develop your career within a globally recognised organisation.

If you are passionate about software quality, automation, DevOps, and cloud technologies - and enjoy working in technically complex environments - we would love to hear from you.

At Siemens, we are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace where everyone feels valued, respected, and empowered to succeed. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives, and believe diversity drives innovation and better outcomes for our people, customers, and communities.

Fibre Optic Splicer – South East Queensland


RAD Communications Pty Ltd is looking for an experienced Fibre Optic Splicer to join our growing team delivering projects across South East Queensland.

Company description:

BCE is the second largest non-Government employer in Queensland and the largest diocese in Queensland. We employ over 12,500 people across our 146 school communities across South East Queensland as well as seven office locations - find your next career opportunity with us in the city, country, or on the coast!



Job description:

About Brisbane Catholic Education
Brisbane Catholic Education (BCE) is a vibrant learning community within the Archdiocese, employing over 12,000 people across a diverse range of positions. As the largest provider of Catholic education in Queensland, BCE is dedicated to delivering high-quality education from Prep to Year 12. Through strong partnerships with parents, governments, and the wider community, BCE fosters social responsibility, self-discipline and Catholic values in all its students. We offer a supportive working culture where care for the individual is an important part of our ethos. Arrive at work every day to a welcoming and compassionate environment, knowing that you'll be supported to take care of what's important to you. Learn more about Brisbane Catholic Education.

About our school
Marymount Primary is a co-educational Catholic primary school which caters for over 1000 students and is located on the southern end of the Gold Coast. We are a predominantly 6-stream school which has an inclusive education policy and is situated on the same campus as Marymount College.

Our aim at Marymount Primary is to provide a warm and caring environment in which parents, staff and students work together to provide a holistic approach to education that encourages personal growth and pastoral care, as well as academic, sporting and arts achievement. We are proud to offer specialised technology, sport, music, drama and French lessons along with daily school officer support in beautiful learning environments with modern facilities. Our community connection extends to Burleigh Parish, with Mary Mother of Mercy church adjacent to our school campus. Explore our school website to learn more about us.

Summary

Drive dynamic and efficient ICT services, supporting the school leadership team to enhance and maintain the smooth operation of the school.

  • Location: Marymount Primary School, Burleigh Waters
  • Start date: Commencing July, 2026
  • Work hours: 30 hours per week (5x days/week)
  • Salary: School Officer Level 4 - $36.98/hour + super (School Salary Scale)
  • Status: Term Time (School term week only) | Fixed-term (Term 3 contract)
  • Ad close date: 12 July, 2026
  • Contact: Matthew Edwards | pburleigh@bne.catholic.edu.au

About the opportunity

In this position you will be responsible for:

  • Contributing to the development, maintenance and operations of the ICT network infrastructure and information databases recommending improvements to systems and processes
  • Providing technical advice, analysis and support to teachers, office staff and students to apply innovative ICT techniques and solutions to meet school requirements
  • Installing and maintaining educational delivery systems in accordance with school standards and policies
  • Providing support on the selection, procurement and installation of new ICT hardware and software to enhance existing systems
  • Supervising and supporting the ICT team development to ensure a high level of service and issue resolution including training, coordination of workflow processes, performance assessment and review.
About you

To be successful in this position you will bring relevant contemporary skills and experience including:

  • Expertise in developing, maintaining, and improving ICT network infrastructure and databases
  • Ability to provide technical advice and support, applying innovative ICT solutions
  • Proficiency in installing and maintaining educational delivery systems in line with school standards
  • Experience in selecting, procuring, and installing new ICT hardware and software
  • Strong communication skills and expertise to help support and develop an ICT team
  • Certificate in Information and Communication Technology (ICT) or related field
  • For this position you are not required to be Catholic.

Eligibility and Safeguarding requirements:

  • Eligibility to work in Australia for the duration of the appointment
  • Hold a Paid Employee Positive Notice Blue Card prior to commencement.
Why choose BCE

Join us and be part of the largest Catholic Diocese in Queensland, nurturing your personal and professional development goals. As a valued member of our organisation, enjoy these and other rewards:

  • Opportunities for career advancement and professional development
  • Competitive remuneration and flexible work arrangements (position dependent)
  • Access to targeted AI tools and training with a focus on innovation and strong ethical standards. Learn more here: The Rome Call for AI Ethics and Microsoft Australia News Centre
  • Salary packaging and salary-sacrificing options
  • Active staff social club and wellness programs
  • Personal support for our employees and families through the Employee Assistance Program.
  • Fitness Passport access - discounted membership giving you flexible access to a wide network of gyms, pools and fitness facilities for you and your family.

Please note applications for this position may be considered as they are received, and an appointment made prior to the advertised close date.

Everyone at BCE models and fosters a commitment to the safeguarding of children and promotes a culture where student protection is everyone's responsibility. The Archdiocese of Brisbane has standards of conduct for workers to maintain a safe and healthy environment for children. Our commitment to these standards requires that we conduct working with children checks and background referencing for all persons who will engage in direct and regular involvement with children and young people and/or adults-at-risk. The organisation is fully committed to child safety and has a zero tolerance to abuse of children or adults-at-risk.

Equity and inclusion are at the core of our Catholic values and we recognise the benefits of a diverse workforce. We encourage and welcome candidates from all backgrounds to apply for this position.

By choosing to continue, you agree to the Privacy Policy (Employment Collection Notice which includes being contacted by Brisbane Catholic Education in future if a similar position becomes available) and to be included in BCE's talent pools for future opportunities. Show Privacy Policy

What’s in it for you?
You will be joining a successful, service-based company with a genuine desire to become AI-native and productise their service. This will have all the thrills and opportunities of a startup-style tech environment without the usual funding risks, as it is backed by a well-established, profitable commercial company.

This isn’t just another principal developer job; this is your stepping stone to keep progressing into higher level roles, where you will have the opportunity to come in, work with strong external vendors and be the internal technical lead. You will have hands-on ownership of consolidating the tech stack while serving as the internal technical champion, alongside an elite external development agency, in building the next-generation, AI-native platform.

If you want to move away from rigid corporate bureaucracy, exercise your architectural muscle, and leverage AI tools to move fast, this is your playground. Your footprint will be on every piece of technology that will be scaled globally.


What you’ll be doing:
  • Architecting and executing a repository consolidation project to streamline three legacy platforms (spanning Magento, marketplace, and .NET/SQL Server environments).
  • Serving as the core internal technical authority, partnering closely with the founders, and a top-tier external engineering agency.
  • "Lead the architectural transition to a proprietary, agentic AI-native technology platform, consolidating legacy stacks into a high-performance, modern architecture
  • Balancing deep, rigorous engineering logic with an agile, AI-assisted development approach.
  • Navigating the complexities of legacy codebases to ensure operational stability while the business shifts toward its new flagship platform.

Experience required:
  • Proven experience as a Lead or Principal Engineer, ideally within a scaling startup or fast-paced software environment.
  • Deep technical software engineering experience, with a strong understanding of the software development lifecycle, architecture, and software delivery
  • Strong vendor management skills—you know how to hold external development teams accountable and extract critical technical knowledge.
  • An AI-native mindset, with an intrinsic understanding of how to leverage modern AI tools to accelerate development workflows.
  • Exceptional problem-solving skills and the autonomy required to excel as a self-directed, principal engineer.

About the company:
You are joining a successful, growing service-based business with three established technology platforms, currently scaling into a proprietary, AI-native SaaS model. They are currently investing in building a revolutionary, AI-native SaaS platform designed to automate operations end-to-end, flipping the traditional software model so that AI runs the jobs and humans assist the system.

You will have true autonomy in this role, supported by a strong, well-established leadership team. With a highly profitable core business and a vision to license this software globally, they are poised for explosive growth from their hubs on the Gold Coast and Melbourne.


Benefits:
  • Employee Stock Ownership Plan (ESOP) opportunities on the table.
  • A clear, unbureaucratic pathway directly into a senior-level management role as the company builds out its localised engineering team.
  • Flexible hybrid working arrangements, with a strong preference for candidates based near the Gold Coast.

What’s next?
This role is being recruited by NTP Talent. All applicants will receive a response. The easiest way to register your interest is to apply using the link.

Company Description

Are you ready to be a big part of something big?

At Machines4U machinery part of carsales, we’re all about making buying and selling a great experience. We’ve been evolving with the new economy to help people choose how they move today and tomorrow.

When it comes to your career, we want to offer meaningful opportunities that empower you to make a lasting impact. But for that to happen, we strive to feel small, small enough to stay agile, learn across teams, and connect authentically with each other.

With a vision to be the global leader in online vehicle marketplaces, we’re transforming how people buy and sell across the world. We discover new ideas and bring them to life. We continue to grow, and we continue to evolve and we’re not afraid to take bold steps to get there.

A career in Software Engineering will see you working on products that are used every single day by our millions of customers all over the world. This position is within our ACIL (Auto, Corporate, Industry & Leisure) team. The team focuses on high availability product for Dealership customers to buy and sell their inventory. It plays a vital role in allowing them to maximise their online customer interaction and sale funnels.

We embrace hybrid working combining the flexibility of remote work with the energy and connection of in-person collaboration. We champion flexibility and offer a range of policies and leave options to support your wellbeing.

What’s on offer

  • Recognition as one of Australia’s Best Workplaces™ by Great Place to Work®, a Family Friendly Workplace, a WORK180 endorsed employer.
  • A highly engaged, collaborative team where you’ll learn from exceptional talent.
  • 24 weeks paid parental leave for primary caregivers, four weeks for secondary caregivers, and six weeks paid gender-affirming care leave.
  • Regular hackathons, continuous learning opportunities, and wellbeing initiatives that support your mental, emotional, and physical health.
Job Description

What you’ll be doing

  • Own engineering execution for Machines4U, from product development and platform reliability to engineering practices and delivery cadence
  • Translate business priorities set by senior leadership into a clear, sequenced engineering roadmap and deliver against it
  • Lead a high-performing, distributed engineering team, and hold the behavioural and technical bar
  • Partner closely with product and carsales engineering counterparts to shape the Machines4U technology landscape and ensure engineering decisions serve commercial outcomes
  • Lead the progressive modernisation of Machines4U’s technology stack, balancing legacy platform stability with migration to scalable, secure and group-aligned modern systems.
  • Oversee key platform integrations and third-party technical dependencies, ensuring reliable interfaces across marketplace, CRM, payments, marketing, reporting and group systems.
  • Own engineering input into security, reliability and compliance practices, ensuring risks are understood, prioritised and remediated pragmatically.
  • Actively contribute to engineering practices, tooling, and culture across the carsales Engineering community
  • Provide clear, credible engineering perspectives to senior leadership, including assessments of risk, trade-offs, and capacity
Qualifications

What we're looking for

  • Proven experience leading engineering teams in a product-led, commercially-driven environment, ideally in a marketplace or B2B SaaS context
  • Strong technical depth across modern web stacks (backend and frontend), cloud infrastructure, and API design
  • Track record delivering practical engineering roadmaps that move the needle on business outcomes
  • Experience operating in a mixed legacy and modern technology environment, with a pragmatic approach to modernisation and risk reduction.
  • Natural relationship builder who can work effectively across functions, holding your own with stakeholders while staying accountable to a broader group
  • Credible operating range: from coding standards and incident response to roadmap trade-offs and senior leadership updates
  • Hands-on enough to review architecture, unblock engineers, and spot quality problems early
  • Pragmatic experience balancing delivery pace and meeting standards for security, reliability, and scalability

We know not everyone will meet every requirement, and that’s okay. If you’re excited about the role and believe you can make a difference, we’d love to hear from you.

Additional Information

A few other things we want you to know...

  • As an equal opportunity employer, Machines4U and carsales welcomes applications from individuals of all backgrounds, identities, abilities, and life experiences. We are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace where everyone feels valued and supported.
  • If you have a disability or require adjustments to participate fully in the recruitment process, please let us know - we’re here to support you.
  • We understand the importance of work-life balance and offer flexible working arrangements, including part-time options. If you’d like to explore this, just ask during the process.
  • We’re also a proud Circle Back Initiative employer, which means we commit to responding to every applicant.
  • Successful candidates joining the Machines4U and carsales team will need to complete a National Police Record check and must have full-time Australian working rights.

So come and join our team - because every role is a big role in our plans to go big.

Be Bold. Belong. Be More.

At Southern Cross University, we do things differently, and so can you.

A career with us is more than you might expect. Yes, it’s about the heart, connectedness and community you’d hope for in a regional university. But there’s also so much more. Our game changing Southern Cross Model brings a deeper, focused learning experience and our research is deeply connected to our regions, making a difference on a global scale. This is your chance to help us build something new and meaningful for our students, our communities and your career. To drive change, make a real impact, and thrive in a place as unique as you.

You can be good anywhere. Here, you can be great.

Your opportunity

Southern Cross University is on an ambitious journey to redefine how technology empowers learning, research, and community impact. We’re building a future-ready technology organisation – modern, secure, adaptable, and driven by innovation.  Our teams are embracing emerging technologies, including artificial intelligence, to deliver smarter services and real-world benefits for students, staff, and the wider community.

Within Technology Services, our five dynamic work units – Data, Architecture & Integration, Cyber Resilience, Digital Delivery & Innovation, Technology Platforms, and Operations & Services – work collaboratively to deliver high-impact solutions across the University.

We’re now seeking a visionary AI Engineer to design and deliver transformative AI-powered solutions that reshape how the University operates, learns, and engages.  This is your chance to be at the forefront of AI in higher education, building intelligent applications, autonomous agents, and automation tools that will unlock new efficiencies and experiences across student services, teaching, learning, research, and administration.

This strategic role reports to the Business Process Engineering Principal and works across a rich ecosystem of university portfolios, from Future Students to Research & Enterprise, building next generation AI systems and driving intelligent automation at scale

You will make an impact by:

  • Design and deliver advanced AI solutions that address real-world challenges across learning, research and university operations
  • Develop and deploy intelligent agents to enhance student services, automate workflows, and support data-driven decision-making
  • Collaborate with academic and professional stakeholders to identify, shape, and implement high-value AI use cases across university domains
  • Create AI powered features and integrations with key platforms like Microsoft Dynamics, TechnologyOne, and Blackboard using Azure and OpenAI tools
  • Champion responsible AI development while fostering innovation through continuous learning, cross-functional collaboration, and ethical practices

What you will bring

You’re a creative and technically sharp AI Engineer who’s passionate about applying advanced technologies to solve complex, real-world problems.  You thrive in fast paced, collaborative environments and are driven by a desire to make a measurable difference in the student and academic experience.

To succeed in this role, you will also need:

  • Extensive AI/ML experience with a relevant degree and ideally 7+ years in development, including 4+ years focused on AI/ML applications
  • Strong technical expertise in LLMs, neural networks, and frameworks like PyTorch, TensorFlow, and scikit-learn
  • Proficiency in Python and .Net, with hands-on experience using Azure AI, OpenAI, Cognitive Services, and Azure machine Learning
  • Proven track record delivering enterprise-grade AI solutions, ideally within higher education or similarly complex environments
  • Deep understanding of ethical AI practices, with the ability to collaborate across diverse teams and drive continuous innovation

Why Southern Cross University

SCU is proudly a regional university, young, small and vibrant. That means you’ll find more exposure, opportunity and ownership - to grow, achieve and build a career that means more. And you’ll do it alongside passionate people and leaders who go the extra mile to help you succeed. A career with us is about more than just work. It’s about place, community and the life you want to live. With three main campuses at the Gold Coast, Coffs Harbour and the Northern Rivers region of NSW, we’re integral to all these wonderful communities. Find your place and be more in this beautiful part of the world.

Your opportunity is:

  • Full-time, continuing (permanent)
  • Located at either Coffs Harbour, NSW 2450, Gold Coast, QLD 4225, or Lismore, NSW 2480. Relocation assistance is available, making it easier to join our regional communities
  • HEW Level 9. The base salary ranges from $134,393 - $140,896 per annum, plus 17% employer’s contribution to superannuation. The total remuneration package is up to $164,848.

You will enjoy a range of perks designed to support your wellbeing, growth and work-life balance. For more information on our benefits and what it’s like to work at SCU, visit Why work at Southern Cross University?

Be more with Southern Cross University today

Southern Cross University values potential as much as experience. If you do not meet all the listed requirements but are excited about the role and confident you can contribute, we encourage you to apply or reach out for more information.

Level 1-2 IT Support Engineer


Arundel QLD | Full Time | Permanent

 

Ready to grow your IT career with a team that actually invests in you?

This is a hands-on support role with a well-established managed services provider serving clients across Australia. You'll work across a varied client portfolio where no two days look the same, and you'll have real access to experienced engineers and leadership who are genuinely interested in your development.

If you're working towards Microsoft certifications (MS-900, AZ-900 and beyond), we'll support you through the journey and help you get there faster.

 

About the Company

This is an award-winning managed IT, cyber security, cloud, and AI solutions provider that has been partnering with Australian businesses for over 20 years. Operating across the Gold Coast, Brisbane, Sydney, and beyond, they serve clients across a range of industries including construction, financial services, medical, real estate, and not-for-profit.

As an accredited Microsoft Partner, they deliver practical, tailored technology solutions from managed IT and cyber security to cloud migration, AI enablement, and vCIO advisory. They're not just a support desk; they position themselves as a whole-of-business technology partner, helping clients turn IT into a genuine competitive advantage.

The team is tight knit, values driven, and genuinely invested in the growth of their people. You'll have direct access to experienced engineers and leadership, work across a diverse client portfolio, and be part of a business that takes professional development seriously.

 

What you'll be doing

Day to day, you'll be the first and second point of contact for client technical issues - diagnosing, troubleshooting, and resolving across a range of environments. You'll maintain strong client relationships, manage your own ticket queue, and contribute to a team that prides itself on proactive support, not just reactive fixes.


Expect to work across:

  • Microsoft 365 and cloud-based environments

  • Active Directory and Exchange

  • General desktop, network, and systems support

  • Client communication and relationship management


What we're looking for

  • 2+ years' experience in an MSP or IT support role

  • Solid working knowledge of M365, Active Directory, and cloud technologies

  • Strong communication skills - you're comfortable talking to clients, not just tickets

  • A genuine customer service mindset - you take ownership and follow through

  • Relevant qualifications (IT degree/diploma) or equivalent experience

  • Microsoft, CompTIA, ITIL, or Cisco certifications are a real advantage

  • Experience with ConnectWise PSA is a plus


What makes this worth your time

  • A collaborative, supportive team environment - your work is seen and valued

  • Direct access to senior engineers and leadership

  • Structured support for certification and professional development

  • Diverse client exposure across multiple industries

  • A stable, established business with a strong reputation


Sound like you? Hit apply and tell us a bit about where you're at in your career and where you want to go.

About Us

Banks Benchtops and Royal Victoria Collection are Queensland's leading stone benchtop businesses, operating across five sites with a team of 90+ staff. We're in the middle of an exciting HubSpot rollout and need an experienced specialist to help us get across the line fast.

The Role

We're looking for a hands-on HubSpot expert to join us on a contract basis. You'll work closely with our internal IT team to finalise our HubSpot implementation across Sales Hub and Marketing Hub, and deliver practical, no-fluff training to our sales and admin staff to ensure confident adoption before key deadlines.

What You'll Be Doing

  • Delivering targeted, role-based training sessions for sales staff, managers, executives, and admin users

  • Reviewing and completing our existing HubSpot portal setup (pipelines, workflows, quotes, properties, automations)

  • Identifying gaps and making configuration improvements where needed

  • Creating simple reference materials (SOPs, quick-reference guides) for ongoing team use

  • Collaborating with IT to ensure integrations and configurations are working correctly capturing the correct information

What We're Looking For

  • Proven hands-on experience with HubSpot — Sales Hub and/or Marketing Hub essential

  • Previous experience training end-users in a business environment

  • Ability to work to tight deadlines and hit the ground running

  • Strong communication skills — able to explain technical concepts to non-technical staff

  • HubSpot certification(s) highly regarded

  • Experience with CRM migrations or implementations in SME environments a plus

Why Join Us

  • Flexible contract scope — we'll structure the engagement to suit the right person

  • Collaborative team environment with direct access to decision-makers

  • Real impact — your work will shape how 90+ staff use their CRM every day
