jobs.job_description:
Level 2 IT Support Engineer – Healthcare IT

Upper Coomera, Gold Coast, QLD
Casual\Full Time | Healthcare Managed Services Provider (MSP)

At Focus I.T. Healthcare Solutions, we specialise in providing IT support and technology solutions to medical practices and healthcare providers across Queensland.

Our philosophy is simple: quick resolution, exceptional service, and long-term client relationships.

We pride ourselves on doing the extra 10% that others don't. It's this commitment to excellence that has helped us become a trusted technology partner for healthcare providers and a workplace where talented IT professionals can build rewarding careers.

We're looking for a motivated and customer-focused Level 2 IT Support Engineer to join our growing team.

If you enjoy solving problems, working with a wide variety of technologies, and want to learn from an experienced team of healthcare IT specialists, we'd love to hear from you.

About the Role

This role combines remote support, onsite support, project work, and customer relationship management.

You will be responsible for supporting medical practices and healthcare providers with a wide range of technologies while delivering exceptional customer service.

This is not a role for someone seeking a strictly 9-to-5 environment. We take ownership of our responsibilities and work together to ensure our clients receive the support they need when they need it.

In return, you'll gain exposure to complex IT environments, healthcare technologies, cloud platforms, networking, cybersecurity, and infrastructure projects.

Key Responsibilities

  • Provide Level 1 and Level 2 technical support to clients.

  • Troubleshoot desktop, server, networking, and cloud-based issues.

  • Manage support tickets through to resolution.

  • Support Microsoft 365 environments.

  • Configure and deploy desktops, laptops, mobile devices, and printers.

  • Join workstations to domains and configure user environments.

  • Monitor and respond to RMM alerts and notifications.

  • Assist with backup monitoring and verification.

  • Support VPN and remote access solutions.

  • Escalate complex issues where required.

  • Create and maintain technical documentation.

  • Deliver onsite and remote support to clients.

  • Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives and internal projects.

Essential Skills & Experience

  • Previous experience working in an MSP or IT support environment.

  • Strong Windows 11 troubleshooting skills.

  • Experience with Microsoft 365 administration.

  • Understanding of Active Directory, DNS, DHCP, and Group Policy.

  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

  • Proven ability to manage a ticket queue effectively.

  • Strong troubleshooting and problem-solving abilities.

  • Ability to prioritise and work under pressure.

  • Current Australian Driver's Licence.

Desirable Skills

Experience with any of the following will be highly regarded:

  • Best Practice or Medical Director software.

  • Windows Server 2022–2025

  • Hyper-V and VMware vSphere.

  • Microsoft Exchange and SharePoint.

  • Mobile Device Management (MDM).

  • Apple macOS support.

  • Datto, Kaseya, and RMM platforms.

  • Cybersecurity solutions and endpoint protection.

  • Microsoft Certifications.

  • Certificate III or IV in Information Technology.

What We Offer

  • Competitive salary package.

  • Ongoing training and professional development.

  • Exposure to modern technologies and healthcare IT environments.

  • Career progression opportunities.

  • Supportive team culture.

  • Company uniform provided.

  • Opportunity to work with one of Queensland's leading healthcare-focused MSPs.

Mandatory Requirements

Due to the healthcare environments we support:

  • Successful applicants must have the right to work in Australia.

  • National Police Check may be required.

How to Apply

To be considered, applicants must submit:

  1. A current resume.

  2. A cover letter outlining:

    • Why are you suitable for the role?

    • Your MSP experience (if applicable).

    • Why would you like to join Focus I.T. Healthcare Solutions?

Applications without a cover letter will not be considered.

If you're ready to build your IT career with a fast-growing healthcare technology company, we'd love to hear from you.

jobs.job_title: Cyber Security and/or Software Development Course Coach
jobs.job_description:
Are you a passionate & caring person looking to develop your training skills in a positive environment?

We are looking for enthusiastic and people-focused individuals that love to share their knowledge and skills with others. Candidates will have great communication skills with a diverse scope of people and willingness to learn and serve students towards a greater understanding. This role is remote and is suited for people all across Australia.


Application Information:

To apply for this role, you must:

  1. Answer the Video Submission Question here - https://vocalvideo.com/c/eqc-institute-trainer

  2. Provide a resume highlighting your experience, knowledge, and skills relevant to this role. (Submit this via Seek)



Job Role:
Provide education and training to students via Online Course Coaching.

Being a Course Coach involves:

  • Tutoring and teaching adult learners. This is done by having consistent communication with students via Zoom calls, phone or written communication.

  • General support and connection with students about course questions and study requirements.

  • Basic scheduling and time management skills are required for a successful candidate.

  • Working with a passionate team to ensure student success.


We are currently hiring course coaches with Cyber Security skills and experience or Full Stack Web Development skills. If you meet either one of these skills please apply below!

Skills Needed Cyber:

  • Experience with Cyber Security Systems and Tools (Basic Cloudflare, AWS, Wireshark, Virtual Machines)

  • Strong understanding of Cyber Security foundations

  • Industry experience is preferred


Skills Needed Web Development:

  • Experience in Full Stack Web Development

  • Proficiency with at least 2 programming languages (HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Python)

  • Experience with Github and SQL or similar database programing

  • Experience with Blockchain and AI / ML is preferred


Universal Training Skills:

  • Strong English Communication Skills

  • Computer with appropriate microphone and camera settings to host a zoom call

  • Flexible availability to communicate with students

  • Valid TAE (training and assessment) qualification - if you do not have this, be willing to obtain the qualification.
     

Salary:

This is a part-time role. The part-time rate for a Course Coach for this particular role is $45 per hour. Successful candidates will be doing 10 hours per week.

This job is dependent on student availability and self-autonomy for scheduling. Hours are averaged across the week, with hours being dependent on student availability.


About Us

We are a Vocational Education and Training Organisation who's team has been operating for over 10 years in this space. We make sure to always focus on delivering quality materials, practical training and always aim to find new ways that can raise the bar across different industries. What sets us apart is our commitment to and incredible student experience whilst studying.


We work together; listening and challenging to create a supportive work environment whereby everyone works together and is equal.


We are hiring ASAP so if you think this role suits you, we would love to get in touch with you.


Work with us, to work together, and as a team let's inspire the next round of industry leaders!

About the role

We are seeking an individual with solid IT knowledge with daytime availability to join our small team at GC Tech Services as an in-house IT support technician. This role involves both on-site work in clients’ homes, businesses and remote support.

This is a highly flexible, casual role that will vary based on your availability and client demand.

Typical hours are 2–5 hours per week, with busier weeks reaching 10+ hours. Typically bookings are made within 24 hours of the client contacting us.

What you'll be doing

  • Responding to and resolving IT related queries and issues in a timely manner

  • Providing on-site and remote support for hardware, software, and network problems

  • Assisting with the installation, configuration, and maintenance of IT systems and equipment

  • Setting up TVs, phones, computers, tablets, Wi-Fi and mesh networks, parental controls, and smart home devices

  • Teaching clients how to use new technology

  • Identifying and recommending improvements to optimise clients’ systems

What we're looking for

  • Strong general knowledge across hardware, software, networking, and security

  • Intermediate-level technical skills in IT support (1–3 years’ experience preferred)

  • Business systems IT support will be highly desirable

  • Excellent communication and customer service skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts in simple, non-technical language

  • Ability to diagnose and resolve issues efficiently and independently

  • Previous experience in residential or small business IT support is an advantage

  • Relevant certifications or qualifications are a bonus, but not required

  • Own car and a valid Australian driver’s licence

  • An ABN (Australian Business Number)

What we offer

  • Competitive hourly rate

  • Flexible working arrangements

  • A relaxed, supportive work environment

  • Opportunity to build experience across a wide range of IT systems and client scenarios

About us

GC Tech Services is a trusted provider of residential and small business IT support, servicing hundreds of clients across the Gold Coast. We pride ourselves on delivering high-quality, honest, and reliable service, and we work closely with local retailers to support their customers.

Apply now

If you're a reliable, friendly IT professional who enjoys helping people, we’d love to hear from you.

jobs.job_title: Expression of Interest - Casual Register for Information Technology Services
jobs.job_description:
·         Competitive hourly rates

·         Vibrant Robina based campus with free parking

·         Collaborative and supportive team environment

 

As Australia’s first private, not-for-profit university’, Bond University based in Robina on the Gold Coast seeks to be recognised internationally as a leading independent university, imbued with a spirit to innovate, a commitment to influence and a dedication to inspire tomorrow's professionals who share a personalised and transformational student experience.

 

Expression of Interest – Casual IT roles
Information Technology Services
Casual Appointments – Monday to Friday

 

Join Australia’s top university for teaching* and enjoy staff benefits including:

·         Free staff car parking

·         Staff wellbeing program

·         Free & Confidential Employee Assistance Program available for all staff

·         Corporate health and finance discounts

·         Onsite Medical Centre and Counselling Services

·         Onsite Sports Centre including gym, fitness classes and pool#

 

About the Positions

Bond University is currently seeking candidates to join our Casual IT Talent Pool, supporting the Information Technology Services (ITS) team across procurement, purchasing, customer service and administrative functions. We are looking to fill multiple roles from this recruitment campaign. This register will be used to fill roles on an as-needed basis, including providing coverage during staff absences and supporting operational demand during busy periods.

The engagement will be on a flexible, as‑required basis, with hours varying from part-time (two days per week) to full-time during peak periods.

 

Key responsibilities may include:

  • Assisting with day-to-day procurement activities including sourcing quotes, raising purchase orders, and tracking orders through to delivery.

  • Support vendor coordination, including communications, follow-ups, and basic issue resolution to ensure timely delivery of goods and services.

  • Provide procurement administration support such as invoice processing, record keeping, and maintaining accurate documentation.

  • Receive, unpack, and record ICT equipment, ensuring assets are correctly logged in IT asset management systems.

  • Maintain accurate asset records including tagging, tracking, stock control, and status updates.

  • Support asset lifecycle activities including deployment, relocation, warranty returns, and disposal processes.

  • Assist with warehouse and inventory activities including stock organisation, audits, and periodic asset checks.

  • Provide general administrative and operational support to the Procurement and Vendor Management team, including reporting support and process improvement tasks.

  • Provide first-level IT service desk support, including triaging requests, logging and updating tickets, and assisting users with basic technical issues to ensure timely resolution or escalation as required.

 

Candidates with experience in the following areas are encouraged to apply:

  • Customer Support

  • IT Service Desk

  • Customer Service

  • IT Finance

  • Procurement and vendor management

 

Please be aware that this is an expression of interest register, and you will only be contacted if a suitable opportunity arises that aligns with your skills and experience.

You know how to sell complex services into SMB and mid-market organisations.

Not products. Not licences. Not simple transactional deals. Services.

First Focus is looking for a proven Senior Solution Consultant who can turn qualified conversations into high-quality commercial outcomes. We are the leading managed AI and IT services provider in Australia and New Zealand, and our marketing engine creates a strong flow of opportunities with businesses already exploring change.

Your role is to qualify well, run sharp discovery, shape the right solution and close clients who are a strong fit for our managed AI, managed IT, cloud, cyber, modern workplace and project capabilities.

This is a senior individual contributor role. It is not a training-ground sales position, and it is not a lead generation role. You will need to arrive with mature discovery skills, commercial discipline and the ability to build trust with executives quickly.

What You Will Be Doing

You will work with businesses typically in the 20 to 200 seat range that are reviewing their IT environment, AI initiatives, security posture, systems maturity or current technology partner.

AI is at the forefront of this role. First Focus is the market leader in helping SMBs do more with AI in practical, secure and commercially useful ways. You will be at the heart of those conversations, helping business leaders move from curiosity and experimentation to clear priorities, sensible adoption and measurable outcomes.

You will lead the commercial conversation from qualification through to proposal and close. That means understanding the client’s business, identifying real pain, testing urgency, mapping decision process and shaping a solution that is commercially sound and operationally deliverable.

You will work closely with technical specialists, but you will own the sales process. You do not need to be the deepest technical person in the room. You do need to know how to translate AI, security, cloud and managed services capability into business value, ask strong questions and keep the conversation moving toward a decision.

You will write clear, credible proposals that help senior stakeholders understand the case for change. You will also ensure clean handovers into onboarding and delivery, because selling something properly includes setting the client and the delivery team up for success.

About You

You have already sold complex B2B services and you understand that services sales is different from product sales. Trust matters earlier. Discovery matters more. The value has to be made clear before the client can confidently move.

You are curious about AI and already using it to work smarter. You experiment with it in your professional and personal life, not because it is trendy, but because you can see how it improves research, communication, productivity, decision-making and client outcomes.

You are likely to be a strong fit if you have:

  • At least five years of consultative selling, solution consulting or services sales experience.

  • A track record selling managed services, professional services, IT consulting, cyber, cloud, systems integration, AI-enabled services or similar complex services.

  • Experience selling into SMB or mid-market organisations.

  • Confidence speaking with owners, CEOs, CFOs, COOs, IT leaders and senior managers.

  • The ability to qualify firmly and avoid wasting time on weak opportunities.

  • Strong written communication and the ability to produce clear proposals.

  • Enough technical curiosity to learn our service lines quickly and know when to bring in specialists.

  • A genuine interest in AI and how it can help businesses improve performance.

  • Commercial maturity, high standards and the expectation that your remuneration should reflect the value you create.

This role is probably not right if your experience is mainly lead generation, account management, product sales or enterprise sales with large internal bid teams around you. It is also not right if you need a highly scripted sales process to be effective, or if AI is something you only talk about rather than actively use.

What Great Looks Like

Within six months, you will be running strong executive discovery with limited supervision, converting a meaningful percentage of assigned opportunities, producing commercially sharp proposals and contributing materially to new recurring revenue.

You will be trusted by prospects because you understand their business. You will be trusted internally because you qualify properly, sell accurately and set expectations clearly.

You will also be helping SMB leaders understand where AI can create real business value, where they need better foundations first and how First Focus can help them move forward safely and practically.

Why First Focus

First Focus is the leading managed AI and IT services provider in Australia and New Zealand, with more than 350 specialists across Australia, New Zealand and the Philippines.

We are at the front of helping SMBs use AI to work smarter, improve productivity, strengthen decision-making and modernise the way they operate. You will have genuine delivery depth behind you, strong brand credibility and a proven marketing engine creating qualified demand.

You will not be building a pipeline from scratch. You will be working with businesses that already know who we are and are actively exploring options. Your job is to turn the right opportunities into long-term client relationships.

Benefits

  • Strong base-weighted remuneration with achievable performance incentives.

  • Hybrid work and flexible hours.

  • Paid study days and structured professional development.

  • Generous parental leave and wellbeing programs.

  • Extra leave for volunteering or community work.

  • Referral bonuses and profit share, subject to eligibility.

  • Access to deep technical capability across managed AI, managed IT, cloud, cyber, modern workplace and projects.

If you already know how to sell professional services, are genuinely curious about AI and want a senior IC role with qualified demand, strong delivery capability and serious earning potential, we would be happy to speak with you.

Job Description

Frank ICT Pty Ltd, trading as Phone Central, is a telecommunications and business communications provider specialising in the installation, maintenance and support of enterprise communication systems across Australia. Due to continued growth, we are seeking a skilled Telecommunications Field Engineer to join our technical team on a full-time basis.

This position requires the application of specialised technical knowledge to design, install, commission, monitor, troubleshoot and support telecommunications networks and systems.

Key Responsibilities

The successful candidate will be responsible for:

  • Design and document business telecommunications solutions, including VoIP, SIP, cloud/on-premise PBX, unified communications, contact centre, internet/NBN, mobile data and LAN/WAN connectivity required to support telecommunications voice and data performance.

  • Assess client sites and existing telecommunications infrastructure, including carrier services, call flows, network topology, IP addressing, routers, switches, Wi-Fi, handsets, cabling pathways and associated telecommunications-dependent systems and equipment.

  • Prepare and coordinate technical implementation, migration and cutover plans, equipment schedules, network diagrams, configuration records and technical documentation.

  • Commission, configure, test, maintain and repair telecommunications platforms and equipment, including iPECS, SIP trunks, VoIP handsets, call routing, IVR, voicemail, conferencing, call recording, contact centre functions and supporting network devices.

  • Monitor, troubleshoot and optimise telecommunications system performance, including call quality, latency, jitter, packet loss, bandwidth, trunk capacity, failover, service availability, extension usage and contact centre metrics.

  • Diagnose and resolve complex telecommunications and associated network faults, including SIP, routing, firewall/NAT, VLAN/QoS, carrier/NBN, provisioning, call quality and congestion issues.

  • Provide specialist technical advice to clients and internal teams, and coordinate with carriers, ISPs, NBN channels, vendors and technology partners on telecommunications design, migration, activations, number porting, escalations, firmware updates, risks and upgrade requirements.

  • Integrate telecommunications systems with supporting business infrastructure, maintain technical records, complete handover and administrator training, support ongoing maintenance, and ensure compliance with technical, manufacturer, WHS and licensing requirements.

Minimum Requirements

To be considered for this role, applicants must demonstrate:

  • A Diploma or higher qualification in a telecommunications or related field.

  • 1 year full-time experience in a telecommunications or related technical role. Strong candidates with 5+ years of relevant experience will be highly regarded.

  • Practical experience with VoIP, SIP, PBX, cloud telephony, on-premise phone systems, business internet/NBN services, routers, switches, VLANs, QoS, wireless networking and IP-based endpoints.

The position is located at Suite 14 , Level 10, 36 Marine Parade Southport QLD.

Machine Learning Engineer

(Time Series & Predictive Modelling)

Build the future of AI-driven manufacturing

Stacked Farm: Redefining the Future of Vertical Farming

Ready to revolutionise agriculture? Stacked Farm is leading the way in automated indoor vertical farming - driven by innovation, efficiency and sustainability. With proprietary automation technology, we've reimagined how leafy greens are planted and harvested: faster, cleaner and greener than ever before.

Recently named 2025 Sustainability Leader by The Australian Financial Review, winning both the Agriculture & Environment category and the title of Overall Innovation Winner. As we continue our rapid expansion with new farms and advanced product lines, we're reshaping the Ag-Tech landscape.

Why Join Stacked Farm?

Working at Stacked Farm means more than just clocking in. It's a chance to innovate, make an impact and be part of something meaningful.

  • Shape the Future – Join a company solving some of the biggest challenges in agriculture.

  • Long-Term Stability – Be part of a proven and well-funded global business.

  • Collaborative Innovation – Work where your ideas matter and creativity is valued.

  • Exceptional Benefits – Enjoy great perks including the best coffee in the area, free on-site gym and parking, regular achievement recognition, quarterly team events and monthly birthday celebrations.

  • Sustainability at Heart – Help reshape how the world produces food — for good.

Please note: Only candidates with full, permanent working rights in Australia will be considered for this position.

Commencement

July 2026

About the Role

We're looking for a Machine Learning Engineer with expertise in time series forecasting, predictive modelling and statistical machine learning.

You'll develop and optimise advanced forecasting, anomaly detection and optimisation models using complex sensor and operational data to improve real-world manufacturing performance.

Working alongside dedicated software and data engineering teams, you'll focus on model development and performance rather than pipelines or infrastructure. You'll also collaborate with machine vision engineers to build next-generation multi-modal AI systems.

What You’ll Do

  • Develop high-performance models for:

    • Time series forecasting

    • Anomaly detection

    • Predictive maintenance

    • System optimisation

    • Operational intelligence and performance prediction

Key Responsibilities:

  • Design, build and deploy machine learning models for complex real-world systems

  • Apply statistical and probabilistic modelling techniques to understand system behaviour, variability and uncertainty

  • Develop deep learning models for sequential and temporal data, including LSTM, GRU and Transformer-based architectures

  • Extract actionable insights from large-scale sensor and operational datasets

  • Evaluate, benchmark and improve model performance through rigorous experimentation

  • Collaborate with data engineers to leverage reliable, production-ready datasets

  • Partner with machine vision engineers to develop integrated multi-modal AI systems

  • Monitor, maintain and continuously improve model performance in production

What We're Looking For

  • Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics or a related field

  • 4+ years of commercial machine learning experience

  • Proven expertise in time series forecasting and predictive modelling

  • Strong background in statistics, probability and model validation

  • Experience with forecasting techniques such as ARIMA, Prophet, LSTM, GRU, Transformers or similar

  • Strong Python skills with experience using PyTorch and/or TensorFlow

  • Experience working with complex real-world datasets, including noisy, incomplete or irregularly sampled data

  • Demonstrated ability to deliver measurable improvements through machine learning solutions

  • Strong communication and stakeholder collaboration skills

Highly Regarded

Experience working with:

  • Industrial IoT, sensor networks or telemetry data

  • Manufacturing, automation, robotics or agricultural technology

  • Predictive maintenance systems

  • Operational optimisation problems

  • Bayesian modelling or probabilistic forecasting

  • Multi-modal learning systems

  • Computer vision integration

  • MLOps and production deployment workflows

About our Organisation 

VENTORA is the name behind iconic Australian brands including A&L Windows & Doors, Breezway louvres, Corinthian Doors, Stegbar Windows & Doors and Trend Windows & Doors.

As an established manufacturer with over 4,500 employees, our vision is to inspire our customers through leading brands, great service, innovative new products and solutions leveraging the full VENTORA portfolio, while significantly enhancing our ease of doing business built on operational excellence.

About the Opportunity

Due to internal promotion, we are now seeking our next Plant Technology Manager to oversee and manage the IT operational technology environment. In this pivotal role you will ensure that all systems are up to date, fit for purpose, reliable, secure and efficient. As the bridge between operational technology (OT) and IT, you will ensure seamless integration, collaboration and alignment of technological goals with business objectives.

Responsibilities include:

  • Oversee the implementation, maintenance and optimisation of IT operational technology systems across all business units, including multi-function devices and barcode scanners
  • Ensure seamless integration between enterprise IT systems (ERP, CRM) and OT systems (SCADA, MES)
  • Support the convergence of IT and OT environments to enable data-driven decision making and predictive maintenance strategies
  • Work closely with cross-functional teams to ensure alignment with operational and business objectives
  • Develop and enforce technology standards, policies and procedures
  • Participate in operational technology projects from conception to completion, ensuring timely delivery in adherence to defined standards and policies
  • Implement robust cyber security measures to protect assets and data
  • Conduct regular testing, auditing and assessments

About You

While ideally you will bring similar experience from a manufacturing environment, what is vital is your natural ability to communicate and work effectively with multiple stakeholders and cross functional teams.

You will also have:

  • Diploma or above in IT (ideal but not essential)
  • A good understanding of networking, cyber security and general IT infrastructure
  • Knowledge of manufacturing processes and related technology systems eg. SCADA, PLCs, MES (highly regarded)
  • Previous experience with remote access systems
  • Previous experience using InTune
  • The ability to work with and support multiple sites across multiple time zones

About the Benefits

  • Competitive remuneration
  • Flexible work arrangements, with 2 days per week WFH
  • Generous discounts on our extensive range of products
  • Ability to schedule leave every year over the Christmas period.
  • Additional paid day’s leave on your birthday
  • Flare benefits program - salary packaging, discounts and premium perks
  • Employee Assistance Program access
  • Career progression opportunities as part of a 4,500-employee organisation

Your Next Steps
